channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Iran-Linked Tankers U-Turn, Zig-Zag
item.description: Two U.S.-sanctioned tankers carrying cooking fuel are U-turning and zig-zagging in the Gulf of Oman and Arabian Sea.

item.title: Burnham to Back North Sea Oil
item.description: Within days of taking office incoming Prime Minister Andy Burnham is preparing to announce new drilling for oil and gas in the North Sea.

item.title: Rystad Outlines 4 Potential USA-Iran Scenarios
item.description: Rystad Energy revealed that it has revised its U.S.-Iran scenario framework 'following a renewed deterioration in security conditions around the Strait of Hormuz'.

item.title: Iran-Linked LPG Tankers Turn Back as U.S. Blockade Tightens
item.description: Iran-linked carriers of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) have made U-turns and traveled in zig-zagging patterns in recent hours as they face the U.S. naval blockade in the Gulf of Oman, after having cleared the Strait of…

item.title: Pakistan Plans Fresh LNG Purchases Amid Strait of Hormuz Shutdown
item.description: Pakistan is looking to buy additional spot LNG cargoes for July and August delivery as the fresh halt to Strait of Hormuz traffic has disrupted shipments from its term supplier, Qatar. The Pakistani government is close…

item.title: Oil Prices Set for Biggest Weekly Surge Since April as Iran War Escalates
item.description: Oil prices were headed early on Friday for a 12% weekly gain, the biggest jump in one week since April, as the re-escalation in the Middle East and the abrupt halt to the recovery of Strait of Hormuz flows pushed crude…

item.title: Oil Ends Lower but Holds Weekly Gains
item.description: Traders balanced profit-taking against ongoing concerns over disruptions to oil flows through Hormuz.

item.title: The No.1 Commodity Story of the Year Is Flying Under Wall Street’s Radar
item.description: Cesium is an “obscure object of desire” in one of the world’s most tightly constrained critical mineral markets, where commercial production has historically come from only a handful of mines. The push to rebuild…

item.title: China Could Be About to Remove Oil's Biggest Safety Net
item.description: The oil market may soon run out of the supply and demand cushions that have kept prices from soaring to record highs during the huge loss of flows through the Strait of Hormuz. The window provided by the U.S.-Iran…

item.title: India, South Africa To Boost Their Strategic Petroleum Reserves
item.description: Back in March, the 32-member countries of the International Energy Agency (IEA) agreed to a record release of 400 million barrels of oil and refined products from their emergency reserves after the Iran war disrupted…

item.title: IEA Predicts First Decrease in Global Oil Demand Since 2020
item.description: Global oil demand could decrease for the first time since 2020, following months of oil trade restrictions in the Middle East and ongoing geopolitical turmoil, the International Energy Agency (IEA) said on 10th July…

item.title: Chevron Moves Closer to Iraq's Biggest Oil Prize—and a Hormuz Exit Strategy
item.description: Chevron is taking another step toward expanding its footprint in Iraq, and is set to sign two memoranda of understanding on Friday that will move the U.S. supermajor closer to developing the giant West Qurna 2 oilfield…

item.title: SLB, Liberty Agree to Form Data Center Alliance
item.description: 'We will help developers accelerate deployment of new data center capacity', Gavin Rennick, president of SLB's New Energy and Industrial business, said.

item.title: Yemen's Houthis Threaten New Oil Shock as Civil War Reignites
item.description: Yemen's Houthi Rebels are threatening to make the global energy crisis worse at the same time that the nation's own energy security is in dire straits. After four years of an unofficial truce and precarious peacetime…

item.title: Eni CEO: Big Oil Bets on Southeast Asia and Latin America
item.description: The oil and gas industry is shifting its capital investments toward Southeast Asia and Latin America due to prolonged shipping disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, Eni S.p.A (NYSE:E) CEO Claudio Descalzi has revealed…

item.title: CB&I wins EnQuest contract extension for Kittiwake duty holder role
item.description: CB&I’s Asset Solutions business has been awarded a contract extension by EnQuest to continue as duty holder for the Kittiwake platform in the UK North Sea, carrying responsibilities through to cessation of production…

item.title: TotalEnergies Sees Strong Oil Trading
item.description: TotalEnergies SE reported another quarter of bumper oil trading after the Iran war upended crude markets.

item.title: Ukraine Hit 2 Large Russia-Linked Oil Tankers in Black Sea
item.description: Ukrainian naval drones hit two large Russia-linked oil tankers in the Black Sea.

item.title: Government Takes Control of British Steel, Citing National Interest
item.description: The government has brought British Steel under state ownership after the steelmaker’s nationalisation passed a public interest test. The Prime Minister said the move “secured the future of British steelmaking”, and…

item.title: EU Sets Target To Double Electrification Rate, Cut Oil & Gas Use
item.description: The European Union has unveiled the Electrification Action Plan, aiming for electricity to account for 46% of total energy consumption within the bloc by 2040, effectively doubling the current rate. Spearheaded by the…
